title: Add a self-contained LaTeX rendering layer
objective: Render explicit inline and display math with professional mathematical typography and accessible source labels.
acceptanceCriteria: The app has a reusable component for inline $...$ and display $$...$$ expressions
Math rendering is bundled locally rather than depending on a runtime CDN
Malformed or unmatched delimiters fall back to readable source text
details: Add KaTeX locally, implement a MathText parser/renderer, import styles, and add visual styling for overflow and surrounding prose.

title: Annotate and wire course mathematics
objective: Make mathematical notation consistently legible throughout lessons, long-form guides, labs, and capstone instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: All learner-facing prose render paths use the math-aware text component
Representative tensor shapes, equations, gradients, probability expressions, complexity terms, and low-rank formulas are explicitly delimited
Ordinary currency, prose, links, editable workspaces, and code-like identifiers remain readable
details: Wire MathText into course-app, lesson-guide-view, capstone-project-view, and lab labels; annotate high-value expressions in course data and guide/project content.

title: Audit mathematical rendering
objective: Prove notation renders correctly, accessibly, and responsively across the full course.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests cover parsing, rendering hooks, annotated notation, and absence of runtime CDN dependencies
Production build, all tests, and lint pass
Desktop and phone browser checks show legible inline/display math without clipping or console errors
details: Add math-rendering tests, run the complete suite, inspect representative lessons/capstones/labs in the browser, and iterate on any layout or parser gaps.
